channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Botswana bid to legalise same-sex marriage draws church opposition
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Botswana churches and cultural groups oppose a gay couple’s bid to legalise same-sex marriage, arguing it goes against tradition and Christian beliefs.
Read More: https://punchng.com/botswana-bid-to-legalise-same-sex-marriage-draws-church-opposition/

item.title: Natasha seeks trauma counselling for rescued Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan advocates for trauma counselling and psychological support for Oyo pupils and teachers rescued from captivity.
Read More: https://punchng.com/natasha-seeks-trauma-counselling-for-rescued-oyo-pupils-teachers/

item.title: NSCDC arrests 671 suspected illegal miners
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NSCDC’s Mining Marshals have arrested 671 suspected illegal miners across Nigeria, with 397 already arraigned, boosting government revenue from solid m
Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-arrests-671-suspected-illegal-miners/

item.title: Tinubu declares end to raw cocoa exports, unveils plan for local processing
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu declares an end to raw cocoa exports, unveiling an ambitious plan for local processing and value addition to boost Nigeria’s economy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-declares-end-to-raw-cocoa-exports-unveils-plan-for-local-processing/

item.title: NSCDC apprehends two suspects for vandalism in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NSCDC Kwara arrests two suspects, Ishaq Kazeem and Abubakar Jubril, for alleged theft and vandalism of TCN electrical materials in Kwara State.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-apprehends-two-suspects-for-vandalism-in-kwara/

item.title: FG unveils advisory committee to review economic reforms
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has inaugurated a Ministerial Advisory Committee to provide independent, evidence-based reviews of its economic reforms, aiming to t
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-unveils-advisory-committee-to-review-economic-reforms/

item.title: Senate identifies soldiers, policeman killed, injured in Oyo rescue operation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Senate has identified soldiers and a policeman killed and injured in the Oyo rescue operation of abducted pupils and teachers. Learn more about the her
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-identifies-soldiers-policeman-killed-injured-in-oyo-rescue-operation/

item.title: 'I don't care!' - McIlroy, Scheffler dismiss legacy interest ahead of The Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy have both dismissed concerns about how their future legacy is perceived ahead of their latest bid for more major success at The Open.

item.title: NANS declares national emergency over dilapidated campus hostels
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NANS declares a national emergency over dilapidated campus hostels, citing threats to student welfare. Discover how this impacts Nigerian tertiary institut
Read More: https://punchng.com/nans-declares-national-emergency-over-dilapidated-campus-hostels/

item.title: Sanwo-Olu mourns Rhodes-Vivour’s mother, urges family to take heart
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Governor Sanwo-Olu commiserates with Gbadebo Rhodes-Vivour over the death of his mother, Nkechi Rhodes-Vivour, urging the family to take heart.
Read More: https://punchng.com/sanwo-olu-mourns-rhodes-vivours-mother-urges-family-to-take-heart/

item.title: PHOTOS: Parents wait at LAUTECH to receive rescued Oyo schoolchildren
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Parents gather at LAUTECH Teaching Hospital, Ogbomoso, to receive the recently rescued Oyo schoolchildren and teachers after their abduction.
Read More: https://punchng.com/photos-parents-wait-at-lautech-to-receive-rescued-oyo-schoolchildren/

item.title: Dettori back on track after Newmarket car crash
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Frankie Dettori was all smiles as he made his return to the racecourse for the first time since a car crash in Newmarket left him in hospital with a broken thumb and fractured ribs.

item.title: Sky-high returns and earnings expectations could mean the end is near for this stock-market bull run
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Never since 1950 have the S&P 500’s price and earnings both been so far above the historical trend at one time, one strategist said.

item.title: FG sets up decarbonisation team to drive cleaner oil, gas industry
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The federal government has established a decarbonisation working group to reduce emissions and promote cleaner oil and gas operations. The post FG sets up decarbonisation team to drive cleaner oil, gas industry appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NSCDC Mining Marshals arrest 671 illegal miners, secure several convictions
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) has disclosed that its Mining Marshals have arrested no fewer than 671 suspected illegal miners, with 397 already charged before the Federal High Court, while several convictions have been secured as part of the Federal Government’s intensif

item.title: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

item.title: Offa Bank Robbery: Saraki’s Lawyers Tell Judge To Recuse Himself
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Kwara State High Court has adjourned till July 21st, 2026 for hearing of the motion on notice filed by lawyers to former Senate President, Dr Abubakar Bukola Saraki, requesting the Presiding Judge over the Offa robbery case filed by the Kwara State Government against him and his successor, Dr. A

item.title: Appeal Court clears way for NBA to proceed with Saturday’s national officers’ elections
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Clearing the way for the ECNBA to proceed with the election as scheduled for Saturday, the appellate court held that the Oyo State High Court, which issued an injunction against the planned poll, lacked jurisdiction to entertain the suit. The post Appeal Court clears way for NBA to proceed with Sa

item.title: GEF-SGP, UNDP Inaugurate Solar-Powered Cold Room To Boost Fish Preservation In Lagos
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP), implemented by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), has inaugurated a 3-horsepower (hp) solar-powered cold room in Sagbokoji, a riverine fishing community in Lagos State, to improve fish preservation, reduce post-harvest l

item.title: Casino Trends: Regulations and what players should know
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: How regulation, mobile design, bonus transparency, and responsible gaming tools are changing the online casino market in 2026 based on data from review platforms like Slotozilla. The post Casino Trends: Regulations and what players should know appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: All On launches the 2026 Off-Grid Energy Challenge to accelerate clean energy innovation in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: All On, Nigeria’s leading off-grid energy access investor is pleased to announce the launch of the 2026 All On Off-Grid Energy Challenge, a nationwide competition designed to identify, support and scale innovative off-grid solutions that address Nigeria’s critical energy access gap. Applicatio

item.title: Reps Drop Own State Police Bill, Advance Tinubu’s Executive Version
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The House of Representatives has rescinded its earlier Constitution Alteration Bill seeking to establish State Police Services, opting instead to consider an Executive Bill on the subject transmitted to the National Assembly by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. According to a statement signed by the Spok

item.title: Tinubu announces 10,000 new homes in 14 states, FCT; says plan will create over 300,000 jobs
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Tinubu announces plans for over 10,000 housing units across 14 states and FCT, with more than 300,000 jobs expected. The post Tinubu announces 10,000 new homes in 14 states, FCT; says plan will create over 300,000 jobs appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Explained: How X’s new algorithm prioritises replies by mutuals over virality
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Social messaging platform X (formerly Twitter) is rolling out an algorithm update that gives greater visibility to replies…

item.title: ‘France has no skin colour’ — Spain apologises over ex-PM’s remarks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Spain has apologised to France after former Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy sparked a racism row by questioning the "Frenchness" of France's national football team ahead of Tuesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final. The post ‘France has no skin colour’ — Spain apologises over ex-PM’s remark

item.title: Balogun expected controversy after World Cup ban waived
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: United States striker Folarin Balogun knew the decision by Fifa to suspend his one-game ban at the World Cup was going to bring "a lot of controversy" and said it brought "nerves" on his team-mates.

item.title: Wolves Leave Arokodare Out Of Pre-Season Squad
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers have left Super Eagles striker Tolu Arokodare out of their pre-season training squad, intensifying speculation that the 25-year-old could be on his way out of Molineux this summer. Transfer expert Fabrizio Romano reported that the Nigerian forward was not included in Wolves&#

item.title: Lassa Fever Death Toll Hits 221 As NCDC Records Fresh Rise In Infections
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) has raised concerns over a fresh increase in Lassa fever infections across the country, with confirmed cases climbing to 922 and the death toll reaching 221 by the end of Epidemiological Week 26. The latest Lassa Fever Situation Report rel

item.title: Bayelsa nabs 10 cult suspects after deadly clash leaves one dead, three injured
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Bayelsa Police Command arrested 10 suspected cultists after an attack in Yenagoa left one metal scavenger dead and three injured. The post Bayelsa nabs 10 cult suspects after deadly clash leaves one dead, three injured appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: ‘They are relentless’: A scammer is trying to change the email on my empty Robinhood account. What do they have to gain?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’ve just been ignoring their attempts.”

item.title: University Of Abuja, NiMet Strengthen Partnership On Smart Climate Project
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The University of Abuja and the Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet) have agreed to deepen collaboration on the Smart Climate Project to make weather and climate information more accessible to students, researchers, farmers and communities. The Vice-Chancellor of the University of Abuja, Prof. Hak

item.title: Ogun Customs Intercepts N4.6bn Contraband, 12 Tonnes Of Cannabis In Six Months
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Ogun I Area Command of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has intensified its anti-smuggling campaign, intercepting contraband worth more than ₦4.6 billion in the first six months of 2026, including about 30,000 parcels of cannabis weighing over 12 tonnes, in one of the Command’s biggest en

item.title: NCC moves to make digital educational platforms data-free nationwide
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Director of Information and Communications Technology (ICT) at the Federal Ministry of Education, Mrs Zainab Suleiman Abubakar, said the initiative aligns with the Nigeria Education Sector Renewal Initiative (NESRI), which aims to improve learning outcomes through technology. The post NCC moves to m

item.title: Nigerian Youngster Yohanna Makes European Golden Boy Top 100 List
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Brighton and Hove Albion’s Nigerian summer signing Zadok Yohanna has been named among the top 100 candidates for the 2026 European Golden Boy award, with the 19-year-old ranked 80th in the prestigious annual rankings. The list, released by Italian daily Tuttosport — the organisers of the award

item.title: Oyebanji installs youngest traditional ruler in Ekiti
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Governor of Ekiti State, Biodun Oyebanji, on Tuesday installed Oba Adewole Oluwatosin Adewale as the new Owa of Ayegbaju-Ekiti in Oye Local Government Area, The post Oyebanji installs youngest traditional ruler in Ekiti appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Lai Mohammed hails Buhari’s integrity, compassion
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Former Minister of Information and Culture, Alhaji Lai Mohammed, has described late former President Muhammadu Buhari as a patriotic, compassionate and incorruptible leader. The post Lai Mohammed hails Buhari’s integrity, compassion appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: CSO Defends Akwa Ibom NSCDC Commandant, Dismisses Misconduct Allegations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A civil society organisation, Ibom Future Leaders Forum (IFLF), has challenged allegations against the Akwa Ibom State Command of the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), describing them as a campaign aimed at undermining the leadership of the command. The group, while responding to wha

item.title: NGO, Foundation Urge Christian Leaders to Lead Fight Against Gender-Based Violence
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: James Sowole, Abeokuta Religious leaders in Ogun State have been charged to take a more active role in preventing violence against women and girls by creating safer and violence-free environments

item.title: Inflation cools to 3.5% in June in relief brought by brief US-Iran peace deal
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Nwebonyi warns students against cultism, awards N100m scholarship to 930 indigent students

item.description: By Jeff Agbodo, Abakaliki ABAKALIKI — Senate Deputy Chief Whip, Senator Onyekachi Nwebonyi, has cautioned beneficiaries of his scholarship scheme against cultism, prostitution and other social vices, urging them to remain focused on their studies. The lawmaker, who represents Ebonyi North Senatorial

item.title: “Be Seen. Be Hot.”: Thousands Turn Out as Infinix Transforms UNILAG into the Ultimate Youth Experience

item.description: The University of Lagos came alive with colour, music and youthful energy as thousands of students gathered for the Infinix HOT 70 Pro “Be Seen. Be Hot.” Campus Storm, an unforgettable celebration of technology, entertainment and self-expression that transformed the campus into the ultim

item.title: ADC rift worsens as faction threatens Atiku with legal action
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: An ADC faction has asked INEC to remove David Mark's NWC from its portal and issued a seven-day ultimatum to Atiku Abubakar. The post ADC rift worsens as faction threatens Atiku with legal action appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Appeal Court judgment: Kaduna ADC faction seeks compliance, urges reconciliation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ibrahim Hassan-Wuyo The leadership of the Legacy faction of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Kaduna State has called on members to comply with a recent Court of Appeal judgment, urging those aligned with rival party structures to embrace reconciliation and work within what it described as

item.title: FG moves to raise N1.2tn through fresh bond offer
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The DMO reaffirmed that FGN bonds are backed by the full faith and credit of the Federal Government and constitute obligations chargeable on the general assets of the federation.

item.title: Mohammed Sani to spend 20 years in jail for concealing information about bandit kingpin
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Furthermore, the prosecution told the court that Sani conspired with Danranmi to receive ₦580,000 for the purchase of 150 rounds of ammunition from a supplier identified as "Chairman," with reasonable grounds to believe the ammunition would be used to facilitate terrorist activities.

item.title: Tinubu Targets 10,000 Housing Units Across 14 States, FCT
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has disclosed plans to deliver over 10,000 housing units across 14 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) as part of efforts to tackle Nigeria’s housing deficit and create more than 300,000 jobs. The President, represented by the Secretary to the Government of the

item.title: Tinubu: Abuja’s 198km Water Pipeline Network Reflects My Administration’s Unyielding Commitment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has said the newly completed 198-kilometre water pipeline network, constructed by China Geo-Engineering Construction (CGC) Limited in Abuja, reflects his administration’s deliberate and unwavering commitment to meeting the needs of Nigerians. Tinubu stated this on Tuesday

item.title: Boston Dynamics tries using ‘robot dogs’ for deliveries
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Boston Dynamics' robotic quadruped Spot has already found work doing routine factory inspections and patrolling the ruins of Pompeii, but what about deliveries?. The company is testing a new conveyor belt accessory that allows Spot to carry packages from a vehicle and autonomously unload them on a c

item.title: World Cup: ‘They’re a pleasure to watch’ – Fernandez names team to win trophy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Paris Saint-Germain manager, Luis Fernandez, has backed France to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Fernandez insisted that the Les Bleus are a pleasure to watch, adding that they have done an exceptional job so far at the tournament. “France are such a pleasure to watch. They’ve done an out

item.title: Court grants ex-gov Ishaku permission for medical trip abroad
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: An Abuja High Court on Tuesday granted permission to the former governor of Taraba, Darius Ishaku, to travel to Arjam, UAE, for a spinal cord surgery. The post Court grants ex-gov Ishaku permission for medical trip abroad appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Government has inaugurated a Ministerial Advisory Committee made up of independent economic experts to strengthen the quality of economic decision-making and improve the implementation of reforms. Speaking at the inauguration in Abuja, the Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of

item.title: PFIPC Scandal: Court Orders Arrest Of Adeyemi Over Failure To Appear For Arraignment
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja has issued a bench warrant for the arrest of the self-acclaimed Director-General of the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, after he failed to appear for arraignment on charges of forgery, fraud and impersonation. Justice Muhammed

item.title: Lewis Hamilton Joins Kardashian Family Getaway, Fuels Romance With Kim
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Formula One star Lewis Hamilton has further fueled romance rumours with reality TV mogul Kim Kardashian after making a cosy appearance during the Kardashian family’s lakeside getaway. Kim gave fans a glimpse into the vacation by sharing a series of photos featuring her four children and close

item.title: Sony delays its new PS5 fight stick
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Sony is delaying the launch of its FlexStrike fight stick controller for PS5 and PC, which had been set to launch on August 6th, to an unspecified date in the future. The company blames the delayed launch on "unexpected production delays." The original planned launch of the FlexStrike would have lin

item.title: Nomad’s high-end phone accessories are up to 30 percent off
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Premium phone accessory maker Nomad is celebrating its anniversary with discounts on its entire catalog of products. The sale will knocks off up to thirty percent off usual prices and will run through July 20th. We found some particularly good deals from the sale we’d like to draw your attention to.

item.title: Wike: How I Ask My Wife To Pray For Tinubu’s Continual Support
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nyesom Wike, the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) and former Governor of Rivers State, on Tuesday, said he relies on his wife, Eberechi Suzzette Wike’s prayers, for continued support from President Bola Tinubu. Speaking during the inauguration of the Water Supply Network to Bwari Sate

item.title: Murder of UK politician Ann Widdecombe was ‘targeted attack’, police say
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Investigators are looking into what motivated the killing and how much preparation was involved.

item.title: Kebbi Govt Denies Giving Eviction Order To Tractors Association

item.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

item.title: Tinubu assented to 72 out of 373 bills passed by NASS since 2023 — Speaker Tajudeen
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: No fewer than 72 bills out of the 373 passed by the 10th National Assembly have so far been assented to by President Bola Tinubu over the past three years. Speaker Abbas Tajudeen, who disclosed this in Abuja while delivering his keynote address at the 2026 Open Week and unveiling the Third Legislati

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The management of the University of Calabar (UNICAL) has dismissed allegations of insecurity on campus following comments by the Secretary of the Niger Delta Activists Forum, Comrade Ogar Emmanuel Oko, regarding the safety of students in hostels, particularly Malabor and Halls 8 and 9. In a statemen

item.title: Gov Eno Increases Students’ Bursary From ₦20,000 To ₦50,000
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Governor Eno also approved ₦1.968 billion for the payment of examination fees for students in public basic and secondary schools across Akwa Ibom State. The post Gov Eno Increases Students’ Bursary From ₦20,000 To ₦50,000 appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Lagos NURTW organising secretary Ajiboye assassinated
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Images and videos that surfaced on social media shortly after the attack showed Ajiboye's white vehicle with multiple bullet holes on the windscreen and other parts of the body.

item.title: DHQ holds retreat for military veterans to foster unity, welfare and national service
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Defence Headquarters held a retreat for military veterans in Abuja to promote welfare, unity, dialogue and continued national service. The post DHQ holds retreat for military veterans to foster unity, welfare and national service appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Delta Records 138% IGR Growth Through Fiscal Reforms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Delta State Government has announced a 138 per cent increase in its Internally Generated Revenue (IGR), with collections rising from about ₦84 billion in 2023 to more than ₦200 billion, attributing the growth to fiscal reforms and economic diversification initiatives under Governor Sheriff Obore

item.description: LAGOS, NIGERIA — itel, a reliable global brand recognized for delivering budget-friendly, high-quality smart life consumer electronics, has officially made a historic entry into the Nigerian home appliance market with the launch of its highly anticipated first-ever Air Conditioner product line. The

item.title: How jihadist groups like Boko Haram use AI for acts of terror
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A report published by Cambridge University features in-depth interviews with dozens of former members of Nigeria’s Boko Haram explaining how the jihadist group has used artificial intelligence to improve its ability to carry out terror attacks since 2023. Experts say they are unlikely to be the only

item.description: The Director-General also lamented the underutilisation of corps members... The post NYSC urges employers to prioritise Corps members’ welfare, security appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: World leaders travel to Qatar to honour Father Emir
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Leaders from around the world have been in Qatar to offer condolences to Emir Sheikh Tamim.

item.title: Meche Oswald: The man in David’s skin
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: It brings me profound joy to write about inspiring human character and rare leadership qualities. Dr. Isaac David Balami is a name that commands attention. Admirers and critics alike are often struck by his unwavering honesty, uncommon humility, and unmistakable humanity. What inspired this piece wa

item.title: Aliko Dangote: Africa’s industrialist-in-chief, By Wale Osofisan
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Africa does not need to wait for its moment. It can build it. Dangote’s story shows what becomes possible when ambition meets execution and when vision is backed by the courage to invest at scale. If one man can shift the industrial balance of an entire region, imagine what ten could do. Africa’s ne

item.title: Lawmakers must avoid self interest in lawmaking – Sani
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Former Senator representing Kaduna Central, Shehu Sani, has urged members of the National Assembly to avoid making laws that serve personal interests rather than the good of Nigerians.

item.title: Youth leader killed in Benue barely 48 hours after suspected herdsmen kill 18
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A youth leader was killed in Ondo, Otukpo LGA, days after attacks in Benue State claimed 18 lives, police and local officials confirmed. The post Youth leader killed in Benue barely 48 hours after suspected herdsmen kill 18 appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NPFL: Ozor switches Kano Pillars for Katsina United
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Chinedu Ozor has teamed up with Nigeria Premier Football League, NPFL, outfit Katsina United, DAILY POST reports. Ozor moved to the Chanji Boys from North West rivals Kano Pillars. The towering defender was a regular starter for the Sai Masu Gida in the second half of last season. His solid defensiv

item.description: When completed, the laboratory is expected to serve as a national hub for agricultural research and innovation, supporting advances in food safety, biotechnology, biosecurity and environmental monitoring. The post University of Abuja, SCRiH sign agreement to establish national agricultural laborator

item.title: Breaking: ‘Fake’ PFIPC DG, Adeyemi arrested, after court issues bench warrant
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The arrest followed an oral application by police counsel, Wisdom Madaki, who informed the court that the defendant had repeatedly failed to appear for trial, frustrating the prosecution of the case.

item.title: Court shifts Accord Party presidential ticket suit to July 15 as INEC joins dispute
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Abuja Division of the Federal High Court has fixed July 15, 2026 for the substantive hearing of a suit filed by Accord Party chieftain, Dr Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim, seeking an order compelling the party to recognise him as its presidential candidate for the 2027 general election and forward his nam

item.title: FG Appoints Abubakar, Teriba, Uwaleke To Ministerial Economic Advisory Committee
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has inaugurated a Ministerial Advisory Committee (MAC) comprising prominent economists, public finance experts and private sector leaders, including Managing Director of Sterling Bank, Abubakar Suleiman; Chief Executive Officer, Economic Associates, Dr Ayo Teriba; President of

item.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

item.title: Tech giants spending $700 billion on AI data centers are recruiting military veterans to fill thousands of jobs
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Mexico: Football Without Fear
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: As Mexico cohosts the World Cup, a mother confronts gender violence and seeks safety for her daughter through football.

item.title: FG to unveil built environment regulatory framework to improve safety, standards
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Federal Government has stepped up efforts to regulate Nigeria's built environment industry as part of measures to improve safety... read more FG to unveil built environment regulatory framework to improve safety, standards

item.title: Argentina’s Big-Game Experience Gives Them Edge Over England – Scholes
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Paul Scholes has identified the key advantage Argentina possess over England ahead of their blockbuster World Cup semi-final clash on Wednesday — and it has nothing to do with Lionel Messi. The Manchester United legend, speaking on The Good, The Bad & The Football podcast, argued that Lionel Sca

item.title: Best performing Shariah-compliant mutual funds in H1 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Notably, the Net Asset Value (NAV) of Shariah-compliant funds rose by 74% to N138.26 billion within six months, largely driven by increased awareness and competitive returns. The post Best performing Shariah-compliant mutual funds in H1 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: How I signed Wizkid through Banky W – Akon
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Senegalese-American music star Akon has opened up about his role in Wizkid's early career, revealing that he signed the Nigerian singer after Banky W introduced the then-rising talent to him during a visit to Nigeria. The post How I signed Wizkid through Banky W – Akon appeared first on Vangua

item.title: Tinubu to deliver 10,000 housing units across 14 states, FCT
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has announced plans to deliver more than 10,000 housing units across 14 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) as part of efforts to tackle Nigeria’s housing deficit and create more than 300,000 jobs. Tinubu spoke through the Secretary to the Government of the Fed

item.title: Ogun govt tasks learners on continuous, effective reading for better performance
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Learners in Ogun State have been charged to cultivate the habit of creating quality time for reading effectively before the commencement of any form of examination, to obtain better performances and come out with good grades. Commissioner for Education, Science and Technology, Abayomi Arigbabu, gave

item.title: FCCPC seals up 3 milk factories in Onitsha, arrests one, confiscate equipment
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Officials of the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission, FCCPC, on Wednesday sealed three factories in Awada area of Onitsha over non-compliance with acceptable production standards. The enforcement team of the commission who raided the factories destroyed freshly produced milk, pack

item.title: BREAKING: ‘Fake’ PFIPC DG, Adeyemi arrested
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Self-acclaimed Director-General of the non-existent Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, has been arrested shortly after a Federal High Court in Abuja issued a warrant for his arrest. The arrest followed an order by Justice Mohammed Umar after Adeyemi failed

item.title: Police bust syndicate linked to N1bn fraudulent vehicle acquisition in Lagos
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Tijani said investigations showed that the syndicate targeted reputable vehicle dealers by falsely claiming the vehicles were for legitimate business transactions or political purposes.

item.title: Olawepo-Hashim: Court Adjourns Accord Presidential Ticket Suit To July 15
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja on Tuesday adjourned the suit challenging the Accord Party’s 2027 presidential ticket to Wednesday, July 15, after the defendants filed additional court processes shortly before the hearing. The suit, filed by Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim against the Accord

item.title: Six workers rescued alive after processing mill collapse in Kano
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Six workers narrowly escaped death after they were rescued alive from the rubble of a partially collapsed processing mill in Gezawa Local Government Area of Kano State. The incident occurred on Monday evening, prompting an emergency response from the Kano State Command of the Federal Fire Service (F
